subject: Project Management
title: The Snowbird Meeting
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Snowbird Meeting.

hook.text: In February 2001, 17 software thinkers gathered at a ski lodge in Snowbird, Utah.

buildup.text: They came from different camps — XP, Scrum, DSDM, Crystal — but shared one frustration.

discovery.text: Over two days, they found common ground: software was about people, not process.

twist.text: They almost didn't agree on a name. 'Adaptive' lost to 'Agile' by a narrow vote.

climax.text: They published the Agile Manifesto on a simple website. It went viral in the tech world.

punchline.text: A weekend at a ski lodge changed an entire industry.

quiz.question: How many people attended the original Snowbird meeting?
